flags.stage_neg1_fallback: stage_neg1 requested but pivot budget exhausted (NEG1_PIVOT_BUDGET=3): Case 6b + regression pattern (a) — same novelty flag repeats across all 3 reviews: the negative-result flagship lacks a named published target that uses the refuted scalar sharp-calibration method. The reviewer explicitly states Abadie2021 and FirpoPossebom2018 do not make the deterministic identification claim being refuted, so the conjecture is targeting a hypothetical method. This is a conjecture-level positioning defect, not a derivation slip — re-deriving on the same .tex would burn budget. The reviewer's repair suggestion (reframe as a positive primitive theorem, OR find a real refuted target) is a re-angling, not a Stage 0 fix.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of synthetic-control treatment effects when the latent factor model generating donor and treated outcomes is misspecified by a bounded sup-norm or interactive fixed-effect approximation error, characterizing the sharp interval of treatment effects compatible with the observed pre-period donor-treated gap and the misspecification budget, recovering Abadie-Diamond-Hainmueller point identification under exact factor-model fit at the trivial bound
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains doc/API.md Section 3 / PartialID substrate, with no existing ThmSmith PartialID proposal for synthetic-control factor-misspecification support intervals; Panel Q1 entries concern FWL/regression-weight kernels rather than Chebyshev partial-ID support intervals.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are NazaretShiBlei2024 on misspecification-error bounds, ZeitlerVlontzosGilliganLee2023 on nonparametric identifiability and sensitivity for synthetic control, Abadie2021/FirpoPossebom2018 on RMSPE reporting, and CattaneoFengTitiunik2021 on prediction intervals. The revised gap is the deterministic Chebyshev factor-bridge endpoint map H^c(P), the scalarization lower bound, and the regular open-set LP diagnostic for when scalar pre-fit summaries are nonsharp."}
seed_list: Prior work AbadieDiamondHainmueller2010 would imply exact factor balance plus exact pre-fit gives a point counterfactual. Our seed conjectures that bounded factor misspecification yields a sharp Chebyshev interval with a row-span phase transition because pre-fit only disciplines post factors visible in the pre-period loading span.

Prior work NazaretShiBlei2024 would predict small linear misspecification induces controlled SC error. Our seed conjectures that the sharp interval can be unbounded without a post row-span or imbalance cap because a hidden post factor direction is invisible in the observed pre-gap.

Prior work FermanPinto2021 would treat imperfect pre-treatment fit as an inference problem around SC estimates. Our seed conjectures that two pre-gap vectors with the same norm can imply different sharp intervals because their signs and directions load differently on the factor bridge.

Prior work FirpoPossebom2018 would calibrate sensitivity/confidence sets to departures from SC assumptions. Our seed conjectures that such sets are nonsharp unless their sensitivity parameter coincides with the Chebyshev support function generated by the full pre-gap vector.

Prior work BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 would imply augmentation can reduce bias from imperfect fit. Our seed conjectures that augmentation can reduce estimator bias without shrinking the partial-ID interval unless it changes the feasible factor-imbalance set.

Prior work AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 would predict low-rank matrix-completion methods recover counterfactuals under favorable error. Our seed conjectures that bounded sup-norm low-rank misspecification yields a union-over-dictionaries identified set rather than a single prediction because multiple factor dictionaries match the donor panel.

Prior work Xu2017 would estimate an interactive fixed-effect counterfactual. Our seed conjectures that factor normalization is irrelevant for the oracle interval but becomes a sharpness issue in the latent-dictionary union because noncompact reparameterizations can change endpoint attainment.

Prior work ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021 would regularize SC and DID to stabilize estimates. Our seed conjectures that regularization can hide rather than remove an unobserved post factor direction because the identified set is governed by row-span visibility, not estimator shrinkage.

Prior work ChernozhukovWuthrichZhu2021 would provide robust conformal inference for counterfactual prediction. Our seed conjectures that under deterministic bounded misspecification the inferential target should be a sharp partial-ID interval because the point counterfactual is not identified.

Prior work DoudchenkoImbens2016 would organize SC, regression, and DID through weights. Our seed conjectures that extrapolating weights change sharp misspecification intervals through the induced rho=(1+||w||_1)eta budget, not merely through variance or regularization.

Prior work Abadie2021 would treat good pre-treatment fit as a feasibility diagnostic. Our seed conjectures that pre-fit magnitude is insufficient because a separate post-row-span diagnostic determines whether the sharp interval is finite.

Prior work AbadieGardeazabal2003 would report a single counterfactual path in a canonical case study. Our seed conjectures that the same design can report an exact interval for each post-period because bounded factor misspecification transforms the case-study estimand into a missing-entry projection problem.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AbadieDiamondHainmueller2010 would imply exact factor balance plus exact pre-fit gives a point counterfactual. Our seed conjectures that bounded factor misspecification yields a sharp Chebyshev interval with a row-span phase transition because pre-fit only disciplines post factors visible in the pre-period loading span.

anchor_paper.author: Abadie, Diamond, and Hainmueller
anchor_paper.claim: SC point identification follows when synthetic weights balance latent factors under an exact factor model.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: the zero-budget ADH corner acquires a sharp interval and a finite-versus-unbounded phase transition under bounded misspecification.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from AbadieDiamondHainmueller2010 because approximate pre-fit already implies approximate post-fit.
seed_details.rebuttal: ADH does not characterize the sharp compatible set or the row-span failure mode when the post loading has a hidden component.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work NazaretShiBlei2024 would predict small linear misspecification induces controlled SC error. Our seed conjectures that the sharp interval can be unbounded without a post row-span or imbalance cap because a hidden post factor direction is invisible in the observed pre-gap.

anchor_paper.author: Nazaret, Shi, and Blei
anchor_paper.claim: Misspecification of the linear SC assumption can be bounded and mitigated with estimator design.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/phase transition, not blocked: it targets a named misspecification-bounds paper and promotes the obstruction to an open class of hidden-factor directions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from NazaretShiBlei2024 because their bounds already quantify misspecification error.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their bounds are not the sharp identified set conditional on the full pre-gap and do not state the row-span/unboundedness boundary.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FermanPinto2021 would treat imperfect pre-treatment fit as an inference problem around SC estimates. Our seed conjectures that two pre-gap vectors with the same norm can imply different sharp intervals because their signs and directions load differently on the factor bridge.

anchor_paper.author: Ferman and Pinto
anchor_paper.claim: SC inference can be conducted with imperfect pre-treatment fit.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: the phenomenon is directional pre-gap geometry, not a generic weakening of fit assumptions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from FermanPinto2021 because imperfect-fit inference accounts for pre-period gaps.
seed_details.rebuttal: They do not give the deterministic sharp interval as a function of the full gap vector and factor-loading bridge.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FirpoPossebom2018 would calibrate sensitivity/confidence sets to departures from SC assumptions. Our seed conjectures that such sets are nonsharp unless their sensitivity parameter coincides with the Chebyshev support function generated by the full pre-gap vector.

anchor_paper.author: Firpo and Possebom
anchor_paper.claim: SC sensitivity analysis can form confidence sets under deviations from identifying assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sharpness-failure, not blocked: the target is a named sensitivity workflow and the obstruction is non-degenerate gap geometry.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from FirpoPossebom2018 because sensitivity analysis already produces ranges.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their ranges are not stated as sharp projections of a bounded IFE feasible set.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 would imply augmentation can reduce bias from imperfect fit. Our seed conjectures that augmentation can reduce estimator bias without shrinking the partial-ID interval unless it changes the feasible factor-imbalance set.

anchor_paper.author: Ben-Michael, Feller, and Rothstein
anchor_paper.claim: Augmented SC reduces bias under imperfect pre-treatment fit by combining outcome modeling and weighting.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: it separates estimator bias from identified-set geometry rather than extending an estimator.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from augmented SC bias formulas because lower bias means narrower uncertainty.
seed_details.rebuttal: Bias reduction for an estimator does not imply a smaller deterministic set of counterfactuals compatible with the same misspecification budget.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 would predict low-rank matrix-completion methods recover counterfactuals under favorable error. Our seed conjectures that bounded sup-norm low-rank misspecification yields a union-over-dictionaries identified set rather than a single prediction because multiple factor dictionaries match the donor panel.

anchor_paper.author: Athey, Bayati, Doudchenko, Imbens, and Khosravi
anchor_paper.claim: Matrix-completion methods estimate causal panel counterfactuals under low-rank structure.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation, not blocked: switching from estimator risk to sharp missing-entry identified set reveals a nonconvex dictionary-union object.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from matrix completion because the missing entry is optimized over low-rank completions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Existing work gives estimators and risk analysis, not the sharp sup-norm partial-ID interval under observed pre-gap constraints.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Xu2017 would estimate an interactive fixed-effect counterfactual. Our seed conjectures that factor normalization is irrelevant for the oracle interval but becomes a sharpness issue in the latent-dictionary union because noncompact reparameterizations can change endpoint attainment.

anchor_paper.author: Xu
anchor_paper.claim: Generalized synthetic control estimates counterfactuals with interactive fixed effects.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Settled-but-unclear scope, not blocked: it probes sharpness and normalization in the IFE partial-ID set, not an estimator extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from IFE normalization conventions because factor rotations are observationally equivalent.
seed_details.rebuttal: Rotations preserve fitted values, but endpoint attainment for bounded dictionaries needs explicit compactness or completion arguments.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021 would regularize SC and DID to stabilize estimates. Our seed conjectures that regularization can hide rather than remove an unobserved post factor direction because the identified set is governed by row-span visibility, not estimator shrinkage.

anchor_paper.author: Arkhangelsky, Athey, Hirshberg, Imbens, and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: Synthetic DID combines SC-style weighting and DID-style regularization for panel treatment effects.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/phase transition, not blocked: the obstruction targets a named regularized workflow and a non-degenerate hidden-factor regime.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from SDID robustness since regularization stabilizes weights.
seed_details.rebuttal: Weight stability does not identify post-period components outside the pre-period factor span.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChernozhukovWuthrichZhu2021 would provide robust conformal inference for counterfactual prediction. Our seed conjectures that under deterministic bounded misspecification the inferential target should be a sharp partial-ID interval because the point counterfactual is not identified.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Wuthrich, and Zhu
anchor_paper.claim: Conformal methods give exact and robust inference for counterfactual and synthetic controls.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation, not blocked: the switch from point prediction to deterministic partial-ID target reveals an identification gap.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from conformal prediction because it already gives intervals.
seed_details.rebuttal: Conformal intervals cover prediction errors; they are not the sharp deterministic set compatible with a stated factor misspecification budget.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DoudchenkoImbens2016 would organize SC, regression, and DID through weights. Our seed conjectures that extrapolating weights change sharp misspecification intervals through the induced rho=(1+||w||_1)eta budget, not merely through variance or regularization.

anchor_paper.author: Doudchenko and Imbens
anchor_paper.claim: SC, regression, DID, and balancing estimators can be synthesized through weighting and regression choices.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: the named phenomenon is l1 amplification of unit-level misspecification in the sharp interval.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from triangle inequalities for weighted residuals.
seed_details.rebuttal: The triangle part is routine, but the sharp interval also depends on factor-bridge feasibility and row-span geometry.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Abadie2021 would treat good pre-treatment fit as a feasibility diagnostic. Our seed conjectures that pre-fit magnitude is insufficient because a separate post-row-span diagnostic determines whether the sharp interval is finite.

anchor_paper.author: Abadie
anchor_paper.claim: SC feasibility depends on data requirements and the credibility of pre-treatment fit.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: it names a finite-versus-unbounded boundary absent from feasibility diagnostics.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Abadie2021 because feasibility already requires predictive fit.
seed_details.rebuttal: The JEL discussion does not state an algebraic row-span condition for sharp bounded intervals under misspecification.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AbadieGardeazabal2003 would report a single counterfactual path in a canonical case study. Our seed conjectures that the same design can report an exact interval for each post-period because bounded factor misspecification transforms the case-study estimand into a missing-entry projection problem.

anchor_paper.author: Abadie and Gardeazabal
anchor_paper.claim: A synthetic control can estimate the economic cost of conflict in a single treated case study.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Application-driven phenomenon, not blocked: it turns a named case-study estimand into a sharp partial-ID missing-entry problem rather than applying a method to a new dataset.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from later ADH theory because the original application is just an example.
seed_details.rebuttal: The interval object under bounded factor misspecification is not derived in the application or the later ADH theory.

proposed_from.literature_map: AbadieGardeazabal2003 (AER, AbadieGardeazabal2003) introduced the single-treated-unit synthetic-control template; open tension is transparent uncertainty for the missing untreated path. AbadieDiamondHainmueller2010 (JASA, AbadieDiamondHainmueller2010) gives the factor-model point-identification argument under exact balance; tension is what exact object remains when fit is approximate. Abadie2021 (JEL, Abadie2021) stresses feasibility and data requirements; open gap is an algebraic feasibility boundary for bounded misspecification. FermanPinto2021 (Quantitative Economics, FermanPinto2021) studies imperfect pre-treatment fit and inference; gap is sharp deterministic identified sets. FirpoPossebom2018 (JCI, FirpoPossebom2018) develops SC sensitivity/confidence sets; gap is a sharp interval from a stated factor-error budget. BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 (JASA, BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021) augments SC to reduce bias; tension is estimator bias versus identified-set width. DoudchenkoImbens2016 (NBER, DoudchenkoImbens2016) unifies SC/regression/DID weights; phenomenon is weight extrapolation scaling misspecification through the l1 norm. AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 (JASA, AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021) gives matrix-completion estimators; gap is sharp missing-entry sets under sup-norm low-rank approximation. Xu2017 (Political Analysis, Xu2017) estimates interactive fixed effects; gap is partial ID under bounded IFE approximation error. ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021 (AER, ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021) regularizes SC/DID; gap is sharp counterfactual interval rather than regularized point prediction. ChernozhukovWuthrichZhu2021 (JASA, ChernozhukovWuthrichZhu2021) gives conformal SC inference; tension is inference around a point prediction versus around a deterministic partial-ID target. NazaretShiBlei2024 (AISTATS/PMLR, NazaretShiBlei2024) bounds misspecification of linear SC assumptions; open gap is conditioning on the full pre-gap vector to obtain the sharp compatible interval.

banked_reason: Stage -0.5 ACCEPT, Stage 0.5 REVISE x3 on novelty: scalar-calibration sharp-bound + generic-scalar no-go lacked a named published target — reviewer flagged Abadie2021 and FirpoPossebom2018 do not make the refuted claim. Conjecture-level positioning defect; math sound.
